failure_surface: Repeated SyntaxError on line 1 indicating invalid code block formatting; agent submitted code wrapped in XML-style tags that the execution environment cannot parse as Python
root_cause: Agent incorrectly wrapped executable Python code in <execute_code> and <code> tags. These XML-style markup elements are not valid Python syntax, causing the interpreter to fail immediately on line 1 across all attempts. The agent did not recognize this pattern as an error condition requiring format correction.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before submitting code for execution, strip any non-Python wrapper tags or markers (such as <execute_code>, <code>, ```python, etc.) so that only bare Python syntax is passed to the execution environment.
evidence: Cell In[1], line 1 shows '<execute_code>' followed by '^ SyntaxError: invalid syntax'
Multiple consecutive attempts (at least 5) all produced identical SyntaxError on the same location
Error message points directly to the opening angle bracket as the invalid character
All solution snippets contain the same malformed pattern regardless of inner code correctness

memory_items.title: Clean Code Submission Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Code intended for direct execution must contain only valid language syntax without wrapper tags or markup.
memory_items.generalization: Always validate that code blocks submitted to an execution engine begin with standard language keywords (import, def, class, etc.) rather than XML/HTML-style tags or markdown fences.

memory_items.title: Early-Line Syntax Error Diagnosis
memory_items.lesson: SyntaxError on line 1 often indicates extraneous content before the actual code, such as tags, comments in incorrect positions, or encoding issues.
memory_items.generalization: When encountering immediate parse failures, inspect the first few lines for non-code characters, invisible prefixes, or structural markers that should be removed prior to execution.

memory_items.title: Rejection Feedback Loop Awareness
memory_items.lesson: Repeated identical errors indicate a systematic pattern failure, not an iterative learning opportunity.
memory_items.generalization: If the same error occurs on multiple consecutive attempts, pause execution to identify the invariant element (in this case, the wrapper tags) rather than continuing to submit similar variants.

edits.content:
## Execution Format Standards
- Submit only pure language syntax for code execution—no XML tags, HTML markers, or wrapper markup.
- Ensure the first line of executable code contains valid syntax (import statement, function definition, etc.).
- When receiving repeated SyntaxError on line 1, audit for hidden prefixes, encoding artifacts, or structural markers preceding the code.
- Treat identical errors across multiple attempts as a signal to halt and diagnose systemic formatting issues rather than continue iterating on content.
